feedback.text: Good day to you! I am here to give you some feedback on this Act: It should include video game ownership of already existing games and content sold within those video games. You may, or may not have already heard of the "Stop Destroying Games" ECI that already passed 1.4 million signatures. That ECI is there to ensure that video game sellers have to have a plan ready to allow consumers to play games after the seller wishes to end support of those games. This sadly has been a pretty common occurence in the video games industry and even has gotten so out of hand that lobbying groups are now trying to mis-represent what the ECI is trying to achieve. One of the companies that is notorious for these kind of practices, Ubisoft, even went so far as to state that "consumers need to get comfortable not owning their games" in the past. Genuinely what kind of statement is that meant to be? I fear for my hard earned money to just be taken by greedy, malicious companies just because they feel like they didn't earn enough money with something. Instead of even trying to improve their product to actually give us what we were promised, they tend to just shut down the entire game, so that no one has access to it. While the creators behind my current favorite game "Helldivers 2" have not made any indications that they would even consider this route, and I do not believe they would do so either way, I fear for the game and others like it anyway, as no matter what the creators may wish for the product, their higher ups may just overrule their decisions. Please take already existing games into consideration for this act. If possible, taking a look at the aforementioned ECI may also grant more insight into this matter.
